items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

image.altText: A large quantity of illicit drugs and tobacco on a wooden table.

items.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into waterways

children.content: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

image.altText: People lounge on the grass next to a natural body of water.

items.title: Neil the seal is back, bigger than ever — and that's a problem

children.content: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land, with a new video showing him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

gallery.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

thumbnail.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

items.title: Serpentine leafminer detected in Tasmania for first time

children.content: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

image.altText: A fly-like insect with wings sits on a green leaf.

items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

image.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

items.title: Why Boag's being bought by another brewer is 'fantasy'

children.content: A union is calling on the brewery's owner to sell the business and brand, rather than close down production in Tasmania. But a local brewer explains why that's unlikely to happen.

gallery.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

thumbnail.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

items.title: Pauline Hanson says vision of 'monoculture' is like the Socceroos

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has likened her vision of a "monocultural" Australia to the Socceroos as Opposition Leader Angus Taylor clarifies that he would still seek "a version" of multiculturalism.

image.altText: Socceroos players sing the national anthem

items.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson taken offline

children.content: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

children.content: Australia's richest person has opened a facility for homeless veterans in a beachside Perth suburb.

items.title: Socceroos should make 'harsh' call for final group game, Schwarzer says

children.content: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play Paraguay and what time is the game?

children.content: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

articles.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?

items.title: Christian Brothers spending $1.7m a week on abuse cases

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work
